name: Teruel Badlands and Arquillo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Paisajes áridos de Teruel & Embalse de Arquillo de San Blas - circular desde Teruel
description: Cycle an easy 13.5-mile route through the arid landscapes of Teruel, past the Embalse de Arquillo de San Blas, with 699 feet of elevation ga
region_intro: Road cycling around Hoya De Teruel features diverse terrain, from high plateaus at 1,000 to 1,500 meters to river valleys carved by the Alfambra and Guadalaviar rivers. The region is bordered by mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Albarracín and Sierra de Javalambre, offering significant elevation gains. Cyclists can experience varied landscapes, including extensive coniferous forests, poplar groves, and unique geological formations like the "Red Canyon of Teruel" and the "Arcillas Park." Best road cycling routes around Hoya De Teruel The most popular road cycling route is Cabigordo Pass and Cedrillas Loop from Teruel, a 51.2 miles (82.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant climbs over mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Albentosa - Arenoso Reservoir Loop, a moderate 37.7 miles (60.7 km) path. This route offers scenic views around the Arenoso Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Teruel Badlands and Arquillo Reservoir Loop, a 13.5 miles (21.8 km) trail leading through the Teruel Badlands and past the Arquillo Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Road cycling around Hoya De Teruel is defined by high plateaus, river valleys, and mountain ranges, alongside sections of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Hoya De Teruel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hoya De Teruel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Hoya de Teruel?
A: Hoya de Teruel offers over 110 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a diverse selection, from easy loops to challenging mountain climbs, with 10 easy, 48 moderate, and 58 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Hoya de Teruel?
A: The region features diverse terrain, including high plateaus at 1,000 to 1,500 meters, river valleys carved by the Alfambra and Guadalaviar rivers, and mountain ranges like the Sierra de Albarracín and Sierra de Javalambre. Expect varied landscapes, from extensive coniferous forests and poplar groves to unique geological formations.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Hoya de Teruel?
A: Yes, Hoya de Teruel has several easy routes suitable for beginners and families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961532266/teruel-badlands-and-arquillo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teruel Badlands and Arquillo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 13.5-mile (21.8 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962199584/vivir-con-el-rio-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vivir con el Río Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another easy option at 21 km. The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros also offers mostly downhill, car-free sections that are accessible for various fitness levels.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling climbs in Hoya de Teruel?
A: For challenging climbs, consider routes that ascend into the surrounding mountain ranges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962073860/cabigordo-pass-and-cedrillas-loop-from-teruel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabigordo Pass and Cedrillas Loop from Teruel&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 82.4 km route with significant climbs. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962138047/climb-to-picon-del-buitre-from-arcos-de-las-salinas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Picón del Buitre from Arcos de las Salinas&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 900 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What scenic natural features can I see while cycling in the region?
A: You can explore diverse natural features, including extensive coniferous forests, poplar groves along riverbanks, and unique geological formations. Notable sights include the striking "Red Canyon of Teruel" and the "Arcillas Park" near Teruel city, an old clay quarry transformed into a natural area. The Natural Path of the Guadalaviar River also offers rugged, picturesque views with hanging walkways and vertical rocky walls.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to visit along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. Teruel city itself is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, known for its Mudejar and modernist architecture. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1263073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teruel Cathedral Square&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6140646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro and Mausoleum of the Lovers of Teruel&lt;/a&gt;. Medieval villages in the Sierra de Albarracín also offer cultural depth to your rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Hoya de Teruel?
A: The best time for road cycling in Hoya de Teruel is generally during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summer can be hot, especially in lower elevations, while winter can bring cold temperatures and snow to higher mountain passes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Hoya de Teruel?
A: Yes, many routes in Hoya de Teruel are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962154820/albentosa-arenoso-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albentosa - Arenoso Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961532266/teruel-badlands-and-arquillo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teruel Badlands and Arquillo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970645961/valle-rio-turia-y-javalambre-circular-through-the-empty-mountains" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle Rio Turia y Javalambre - circular through the empty mountains&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How can I access road cycling routes in Hoya de Teruel using public transport?
A: While specific public transport options directly to trailheads may vary, the city of Teruel serves as a central hub with bus and train connections. From Teruel, you can often start routes directly or find local transport to nearby villages that serve as starting points. The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, for instance, has accessible points from various towns along its length.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is increasingly catering to cyclists. Many towns and villages along the routes offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. Some hotels provide specialized services like bicycle transport, luggage transfer, and secure storage. Specialized shops like Surya Bikepacking & Yoga Studio also offer equipment and support.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Hoya de Teruel?
A: The road cycling routes in Hoya de Teruel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from scenic river valleys to challenging mountain passes, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with cultural exploration.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in Hoya de Teruel?
A: Yes, Hoya de Teruel offers options for long-distance road cycling. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31440893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos de las Salinas – Source of the Arcos River loop from Sarrión&lt;/a&gt; cover over 100 km and feature significant elevation gains, providing a substantial challenge for experienced riders.

Q: Are there any specific greenways or car-free routes for road cyclists?
A: The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros is a major highlight for cyclists in the region. This greenway follows an old railway line, providing a safe, car-free route that is mostly downhill. It spans three provinces, offering changing landscapes and passing through tunnels and over impressive viaducts like the Albentosa viaduct. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963257141/la-conquista-and-ojos-negros-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Conquista and Ojos Negros Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route incorporating this greenway.
tips: This area is one of the examples of how the river crossing has created small valleys surrounded by hills without vegetation, forming areas of BadLands.
The Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir is built in the Guadalaviar River bed, northwest of the city of Tarol, where it later joins the Túria River.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: This circular road cycling route is designed to be an easy experience, typically taking around 1 hour and 5 minutes to complete. It's suitable for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through arid landscapes characterized by reddish clay terrain and unique geological formations carved by erosion. You'll find tracks of various colored clays. A rural road to San Blas has been recently asphalted, providing a quiet and smooth stretch for cyclists. The trail is primarily suitable for road bikes.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience dramatic arid scenery with unique geological formations and varied clay colors. Key sights include the View of the Badlands and the beautiful Embalse Arquillo de San Blas. You can also enjoy views of the Guadalaviar River canyon and, from certain points, the city of Teruel itself.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as an "easy road bike route" by Komoot. It's relatively short, with a moderate elevation gain, and features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it suitable for beginners and cyclists of any fitness level.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The area is known for its diverse wildlife, particularly birds of prey. Keep an eye out for species such as the golden eagle, Egyptian vulture, and bearded vulture, especially around the canyon areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the cycling route?
faqs.answer: Since the route is circular and starts and ends in Teruel, you can conveniently find parking within the city. There are various public parking options available in Teruel from which you can begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the arid landscapes, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for cycling, offering milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be chilly, so plan accordingly and check the weather forecast.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is open, specific regulations regarding dogs on cycling paths or around the Embalse de Arquillo de San Blas may vary. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife, and ensure they are well-behaved around other cyclists and pedestrians.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and natural areas that are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with parts of the Camino Natural del Guadalaviar, offering a glimpse into this larger natural path.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities along the way?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Teruel, a city with plenty of cafes, restaurants, and other amenities. While the rural sections of the route are quiet, you can easily find refreshments and services before and after your ride in Teruel.
